items.title: Provably optimal linear models
items.description: Convex losses (e.g., squared error in linear regression, cross-entropy in logistic regression) let teams trust that gradient descent reaches the global optimum—useful baselines before adding non-convex deep architectures.

items.title: Explaining neural training behavior
items.description: Non-convexity explains why pretraining, warm restarts, learning-rate schedules, and multiple random seeds are standard practice: they help search a rugged landscape rather than assuming one global bowl.

items.title: Optimizer and architecture design
items.description: Techniques like momentum, Adam, batch normalization, and residual connections are partly motivated by navigating saddles and ill-conditioned valleys in non-convex losses.

items.title: When guarantees matter in production
items.description: Safety-critical or compliance-sensitive pipelines sometimes restrict models to convex formulations (GLMs, convex surrogates) when a certifiable global optimum is required instead of a heuristic non-convex fit.
